title: Data minimization and purpose limitation
content: A health AI system should clearly justify what data it collects and avoid collecting sensitive information that is not necessary for the intended function.

title: Retention and user control
content: Good privacy practice includes a clearly stated retention period and a visible way for users to opt out or request data deletion where appropriate.

title: Capability boundaries
content: Users should be able to understand what the system can do, what it cannot do, and when human support is needed.

title: Reasoning basis and traceability
content: Systems should communicate the basis of recommendations at an understandable level, especially when advice may influence health-related decisions.

title: Group testing
content: Fairness review should include testing across user groups, especially where access, demographic background, or language differences may influence outputs.

title: Monitoring and appeal pathways
content: After deployment, teams should monitor disparate impact and give users a path to correction, appeal, or human review.
